name: Akira Ramen & Izakaya

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled to a current product name in the restaurant-linked MealKeyway response captured on 2026-07-14.
The official site identifies the Rockville restaurant at 1800 Rockville Pike H1, Rockville, MD 20852 and directly links merchant 6c567833364f776d74724c4d686e4e5270446f542b513d3d.

MealKeyway exposes 83 non-hidden rows; two are standalone sauce add-ons, and two Tuna Ikura rows in the same category and price collapse to one better-described formulation, yielding 80 canonical products across ten categories.
The current catalog adds Coke, Diet Coke, Sprite, Ginger Ale, Lunch Special, Tuesday Combo, and a separately described second King Dragon formulation beyond the 73 frozen formulations.
Hidden merchant rows, internal combo choices, alcohol, toppings, and sold-out legacy products are not promoted to public menu products.
The restaurant-linked merchant response publishes fixed descriptions for many products but no complete allergen matrix, complete recipes, or cross-contact policy.
All 73 frozen rows had unavailable allergen data even though current fixed text supports positive signals for 65 of them; the other eight remain accurately unavailable.
The corrected 80-product catalog has 66 positive ingredient-signal rows and 14 unavailable rows; the additional positive row is the second separately described King Dragon formulation.
Crabstick is interpreted as fish rather than shellfish, while explicit crabmeat remains shellfish; configurable lunch and Tuesday package choices are not promoted to fixed allergens.
Absent descriptions are never treated as allergen-free claims, and no may-contain or shared-equipment claims are invented.
The frozen generic extraction retained 73 public names and broad categories but discarded all live merchant descriptions and therefore every available ingredient signal.
It also omitted public non-alcoholic drinks and configurable package products and did not preserve the current duplicate/add-on distinction.

The correction parses the restaurant-linked structured merchant response, filters hidden/internal/alcohol/topping rows, excludes two sauce add-ons, and collapses only the same-category duplicate Tuna Ikura row.

Positive mappings are constrained to fixed published ingredients and unavoidable named formats, with focused tests for surimi, fish cake, tempura/noodle formats, soy, sesame, and configurable packages.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Captured the official site, confirmed the Rockville identity/address, and verified its direct MealKeyway merchant link.
Captured the current MealKeyway customer shell used by the restaurant's online-order flow.
Captured and exhaustively reviewed the structured current merchant catalog, including all categories, visibility fields, descriptions, combo rows, and duplicate rows.
Found current item descriptions but no complete restaurant allergen matrix, recipe disclosure, or cross-contact policy.

findings.summary: The frozen catalog marked every Akira row unavailable even though the current linked merchant response supplies fixed ingredient text supporting positive signals for 65 of the 73 frozen products.
findings.resolution: Parsed the structured current descriptions and recomputed only supported positive signals while retaining eight frozen and 14 total current products as unavailable.

findings.summary: The frozen 73-row catalog omitted current drinks, configurable package products, and one separately described King Dragon formulation while lacking explicit duplicate/add-on handling.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 80 canonical current products across ten categories from all 83 non-hidden rows, excluding two add-ons and collapsing one same-category Tuna Ikura duplicate.

findings.summary: The generic extraction retained names but discarded current merchant descriptions and visibility/combination semantics, causing both incomplete menu coverage and wholesale allergen underreporting.
findings.resolution: Prepared a structured merchant-catalog correction with explicit public-row, hidden-row, add-on, duplicate, and allergen-semantic invariants.

repairs.summary: Prepared and focused-tested an 80-product Akira catalog across ten current categories with 66 supported positive rows, 14 unavailable rows, complete current public coverage, and explicit hidden/add-on/duplicate handling.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=/tmp/akira-ramen-repository.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (isolated copy; verified 80 items, ten categories, 66 positive rows, 14 unavailable rows, and all known hidden/add-on/duplicate invariants)

notes: Current corrected catalog counts: 83 non-hidden merchant rows, 80 canonical products, ten categories, 66 positive rows, and 14 unavailable rows.
